seo.title: PDF too large to upload - Fix file size before sending
seo.description: When a PDF is too large for an application or form, use browser tools to check size, compress, remove pages, and review upload readiness.

lead: Use a simple checklist to reduce size, check the result, and prepare the file for the target platform.

strings.ctaTitle: Check the target before changing the file
strings.ctaText: Upload platforms can reject files for size, page count, format, or readability. Start with a preparation flow, then use compression or page cleanup as needed.
strings.toolCardText: Choose a target size, preview the result, and keep review reminders visible.
strings.stepsTitle: What to do when a PDF is too large
strings.stepsLead: Move from diagnosis to the smallest safe change.
strings.stepOneText: Confirm the exact size limit and whether the platform also has page or format rules.
strings.stepTwoText: Compress the PDF, remove blank pages, or rotate and organize pages before another attempt.
strings.stepThreeText: Open the output and verify readability, page count, and final file size.
strings.limitationsTitle: A rejected upload can have more than one cause
strings.limitationsText: Size is common, but browser memory, encrypted PDFs, page orientation, or platform rules can also block submission.
strings.limitationsItemOne: A file may be under the size limit but still fail because it is encrypted or malformed.
strings.limitationsItemTwo: Large scans can exceed browser memory during processing.
strings.limitationsItemThree: Some platforms require a specific file type, page count, or readable resolution.
strings.privacyText: The selected PDF is read by your browser to create results. The page may still request normal site resources, analytics, and ads.
strings.privacyItemOneText: Save a copy of the original before replacing it with a smaller upload version.
strings.privacyItemTwoText: For IDs, visas, or application documents, confirm every page before submitting.
strings.relatedLead: Choose the next tool based on why the file failed.
strings.relatedCompressText: Reduce size when the file exceeds the upload cap.
strings.relatedUploadText: Use target-size checks and preview reminders.
strings.relatedManageText: Delete extra pages or fix orientation before uploading again.
strings.faqOneQuestion: Why does my PDF upload fail even after compression?
strings.faqOneAnswer: The platform may also check page count, encryption, file structure, or readability, not only size.
strings.faqTwoQuestion: Should I remove pages before compressing?
strings.faqTwoAnswer: If pages are unnecessary, removing them first can be more reliable than compression alone.
strings.faqThreeQuestion: Can I prepare files on a phone?
strings.faqThreeAnswer: Small files may work, but a desktop browser is safer for important PDFs because mobile memory is limited.
